title: ophrys ferrum-equinum subsp. ferrum-equinum
intro: Ophrys ferrum-equinum subsp. ferrum-equinum, also known as the Iron Horse Orchid, is a rare and unique subspecies of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Ophrys ferrum-equinum subsp. ferrum-equinum include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing its roots and leaves, which can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
